title: What to know about Salvia officinalis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Salvia officinalis, commonly known as common sage, is a perennial herb used for medicinal, culinary, and ornamental purposes. Its main medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, as well as alleviating symptoms of menopause and hot flashes. The herb's main health benefits include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects. Salvia officinalis has main therapeutic properties of antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral action, as well as vasodilatory and antioxidant effects. The herb's main bioactive compounds include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and rosmarinic acid, which are commonly used to make tea, infusions, and extracts for medicinal purposes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, an ethanolic extract of Zingiber officinale has been found to exhibit several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and fever. Additionally, it has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, effectively inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, the extract has been found to have hypoglycaemic properties, resulting in blood glucose lowering in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Salvia officinalis are the relief of digestive issues, such as indigestion, due to the presence of carvacrol, a compound that exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil of Salvia officinalis has also been found to effectively treat respiratory problems, including cough and respiratory issues, by inhibiting the production of mucus and reducing inflammation. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been used to alleviate anxiety, fever, and inflammation, likely due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its content of borneol, make it a potential treatment for diarrhea and skin conditions, such as eczema, by soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, likely due to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.

uses_list.description: This plant has been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and gas in the digestive tract.

The essential oil of this plant contains compounds that have been shown to have anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by binding to GABA receptors in the brain and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters.

This plant has been used to relieve symptoms of coughs, such as bronchitis and whooping cough, due to its expectorant properties, which help to thin and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.

The plant has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to thin and expel mucus from the airways and reduce inflammation.

This plant has been used to relieve symptoms of diarrhea, such as loose stools and abdominal cramps, due to its antispasmodic and astringent properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and slow down bowel movements.

The plant has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to thin and expel mucus from the airways and reduce inflammation.

This plant has been used to relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and heartburn, due to its antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and gas in the digestive tract.

The plant has been traditionally used to reduce fever and relieve symptoms of infections, such as sweating and chills, due to its antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms.

This plant has been used to relieve symptoms of inflammation, such as redness and swelling, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection.

The plant has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various health conditions, primarily digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the gastrointestinal tract. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been studied for its potential anxiolytic effects, suggesting its efficacy in managing anxiety disorder symptoms, particularly generalized anxiety disorder. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties have been shown to aid in wound healing by reducing the risk of infection. The herb has also been investigated for its potential applications in treating skin conditions, such as acne, by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth. Moreover, research has explored its effects on diabetes, gastrointestinal issues, respiratory problems, inflammation, migraine, and antimicrobial therapy, highlighting its potential as a versatile treatment option.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various ailments. Salvia officinalis is often applied topically to relieve digestive issues such as indigestion, and its anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's antipyretic properties make it a treatment option for fever, and its expectorant properties help ease cough and respiratory issues. Additionally, Salvia officinalis is used to treat diarrhea, anxiety, and nausea, often in conjunction with other herbs. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help alleviate inflammation, and its calming effects can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various health conditions. The plant's carvacrol content helps alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, by regulating gut bacteria. Its antimicrobial properties also address respiratory problems, including bronchitis and pneumonia, by reducing inflammation and killing pathogens. Additionally, Salvia officinalis is used to soothe coughs and relieve skin conditions like eczema and acne. Furthermore, it is known to treat diarrhea, anxiety, wounds, menstrual issues, respiratory infections, and inflammation, showcasing its broad therapeutic applications.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat digestive issues such as indigestion and dyspepsia by enhancing digestive enzymes and alleviating symptoms like diarrhea. Its expectorant properties also make it effective in treating cough and respiratory issues, including respiratory problems like asthma, by loosening mucus and facilitating easier breathing. Additionally, it is used to reduce fever and alleviate anxiety by modulating the body's response to stress and infection. The plant's carminative properties also help in relieving hiccups and promoting a sense of calm. Furthermore, its antispasmodic and expectorant properties make it useful in treating respiratory issues, including bronchitis and other respiratory problems.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues, such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. The essential oils extracted from Salvia officinalis have also been used to alleviate anxiety and stress-related disorders. Furthermore, the plant has been found to be effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and coughing. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been used to improve memory loss and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties also make it useful in treating inflammation, insomnia, skin conditions, sleep disorders, and migraines.
benefits_description: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines through its polyphenolic compounds, which also antagonize insulin resistance by enhancing glucose uptake in skeletal muscle cells. Lowering blood pressure is achieved through the potassium content in Salvia officinalis, which helps counterbalance the effects of sodium and promote vasodilation. Improving cognitive function is attributed to the neuroprotective properties of Salvia officinalis, particularly its ability to reduce the risk of Alzheimer's by inhibiting the aggregation of amyloid-beta peptides. Relieving anxiety symptoms is facilitated by the anxiolytic effects of Salvia officinalis, which are mediated by the modulation of GABA receptors and the reduction of free radical production, thus preventing cancer cell growth by inducing apoptosis. Additionally, Salvia officinalis promotes wound healing by stimulating the production of growth factors and protecting against bacterial infections through its antimicrobial properties, including its ability to fight against free radicals.

benefits_list.description: This herb has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, a polyphenol that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation in the body.
The herb contains compounds that relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which in turn helps to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
This herb has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory due to its ability to increase blood flow to the brain and protect against age-related cognitive decline.
The herb has anxiolytic properties, which means it can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by interacting with neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA and serotonin.
The herb contains antioxidants and polyphenols that can help to prevent cancer cell growth by protecting against DNA damage and inhibiting the growth of cancer cells.
The herb has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce insulin resistance, which can help to prevent the development of type 2 diabetes and other metabolic disorders.
The herb has been shown to reduce the risk of Alzheimer's disease by protecting against age-related cognitive decline and improving memory and cognitive function.
The herb has antimicrobial properties, which means it can help to protect against bacterial infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing the risk of infection.
The herb has been shown to promote wound healing by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and protecting against infection.
The herb contains antioxidants that can help to fight against free radicals, which are unstable molecules that can damage cells and contribute to aging and disease.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Salvia officinalis improves blood flow by containing flavonoids, which act as vasodilators, and also lowers blood pressure due to its antioxidant properties that protect against endothelial dysfunction. Reducing inflammation is another key benefit of Salvia officinalis, thanks to its anti-inflammatory compounds like carnosic acid, which also enhances circulation by improving the functioning of blood vessels. Furthermore, the plant's ability to prevent blood clots is attributed to its antimicrobial properties, which reduce the formation of thrombin and fibrin. Salvia officinalis also boosts cardiac output by increasing the activity of the sinoatrial node and enhancing the contractility of cardiac muscle, while relaxing arteries is a result of its vasodilatory effects that reduce peripheral resistance. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties protect the vasculature, increase oxygenation of tissues, and improve vascular function, making it an effective herb for maintaining a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate digestive discomfort. This herb improves digestion by modulating gut motility and enhancing the secretion of digestive enzymes, ultimately leading to better nutrient absorption. Its antimicrobial properties relax muscles by reducing spasmodic contractions in the gastrointestinal tract, while also soothing irritation caused by pathogens and toxins. The antioxidant properties in Salvia officinalis fight infections by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress in the digestive system, which in turn eases nausea caused by inflammation and irritation. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, Salvia officinalis prevents diarrhea, calms the gut, stimulates appetite, and heals ulcers, ultimately maintaining a balanced digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Salvia officinalis regulates blood sugar through its ability to reduce glucose uptake in the intestines and improve insulin sensitivity, attributed to its antioxidant properties. Stabilizing insulin levels is also essential, which Salvia officinalis achieves through its anti-inflammatory action, reducing insulin resistance and promoting glucose metabolism. Balancing thyroid hormones is a significant health benefit of Salvia officinalis, as its antimicrobial properties help prevent the invasion of pathogens that can disrupt thyroid function. Suppressing cortisol production is another key benefit, attributed to Salvia officinalis's anti-stress properties, which reduce cortisol levels and alleviate anxiety. Additionally, Salvia officinalis enhances adrenal function, boosts testosterone levels, regenerates pancreatic cells, reduces inflammation quickly, improves metabolic rate, and releases human growth hormone, all of which are linked to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and adaptogenic properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate redness and swelling in the integumentary system. The antioxidant properties of Salvia officinalis protect skin cells from oxidative damage, preventing premature aging and skin damage. By possessing antimicrobial properties, Salvia officinalis heals wound faster by preventing bacterial infections from taking hold. Additionally, its antiseptic properties repair damaged tissue by promoting a clean environment for the skin to regenerate. Salvia officinalis soothes sunburned skin, fights bacterial infections, stimulates collagen production, improves skin elasticity, reduces oxidative stress, and promotes skin regeneration due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Salvia officinalis reduces swelling by containing anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, which inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. This reduction in inflammation also boosts circulation by promoting vasodilation, allowing for the efficient transport of nutrients and oxygen to tissues. Salvia officinalis stimulates lymph by stimulating the lymphatic system's natural response to infection and injury, which in turn improves flow through the lymphatic vessels. By increasing filtration, Salvia officinalis enhances absorption of nutrients and removal of waste products, while reducing toxins by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. The herb's ability to support drainage, activate lymph nodes, and promote detoxification rounds out its beneficial effects on the lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Salvia officinalis relieve muscle pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, while reducing joint inflammation and easing joint stiffness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The antioxidant and flavonoid compounds in Salvia officinalis improve bone density and strengthen tendons, as well as promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and tissue repair. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also prevent muscle cramps and soothe muscle spasms, reducing muscle fatigue and improving bone strength. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been shown to promote collagen synthesis and tissue repair, which is essential for the repair and regeneration of bone and connective tissue. By reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, Salvia officinalis helps to alleviate various musculoskeletal issues.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Salvia officinalis calms nervous system through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. This calming effect on the nervous system also reduces anxiety levels, allowing individuals to feel more at ease and composed. Additionally, Salvia officinalis stabilizes mood swings by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating mood. Its anti-anxiety properties also relax muscle tension and improve sleep quality by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. Furthermore, the neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing properties of Salvia officinalis enhance cognitive function, boost memory capacity, improve focus abilities, and regulate neurotransmitters, ultimately reducing the stress response.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Salvia officinalis regulates hormones by modulating the endocrine system, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Stimulated fertility in both men and women is attributed to its antioxidant activity, which protects sperm and egg cells from oxidative damage. Increased ovulation is facilitated by the plant's anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the ovaries and improve the release of eggs. Enhanced libido is promoted by the plant's antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which improve blood flow to the genital area. The plant's ability to improve menstruation, boost progesterone, support egg health, reduce infertility, promote reproductive function, and enhance sexual function is due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which maintain the delicate balance of reproductive hormones and protect the reproductive system from damage.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Salvia officinalis relieve congestion by containing salvenol, a compound that acts as a bronchodilator, widening air passages and improving respiratory function. The antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis reduce inflammation in the airways, making it an effective treatment for respiratory issues. Salvia officinalis prevent allergies by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions. The plant's antiviral and antibacterial properties alleviate coughs by eradicating the underlying cause of the cough, while its expectorant properties clear mucus from the airways, soothing bronchitis and easing asthma symptoms. By killing viruses and bacteria, Salvia officinalis helps to prevent respiratory infections, making it a valuable natural remedy for respiratory health.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Salvia officinalis prevents bladder infections and inflammation due to its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that cause infections. Reducing incontinence is also achieved through the plant's ability to relax urinary muscles, thereby increasing bladder control. Salvia officinalis treats kidney stones by reducing pain and inflammation, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties, and soothing prostate issues by modulating hormone production. The plant's antioxidant properties improve kidney function by protecting the kidneys from oxidative damage and eliminating bacterial infections that can cause kidney problems. Salvia officinalis enhances urine flow and protects bladder walls from damage caused by frequent urination and bacterial infections, ultimately curing urinary tract infections.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is largely due to its bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and saponins. Its antioxidant properties, resulting from the presence of volatile oils, help protect cells from oxidative damage and contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects, which are further enhanced by the plant's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the analgesic properties of Salvia officinalis, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, play a significant role in pain relief, while its antispasmodic properties, resulting from the action of volatile oils, help alleviate muscle spasms. The plant's antihistamine properties, which are largely due to the presence of flavonoids, help alleviate allergic reactions, and its cardiovascular protector effects, resulting from the action of flavonoids and saponins, help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Salvia officinalis's neuroprotective properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the brain from oxidative damage and may help alleviate neurodegenerative diseases.

properties_list.description: Salvia officinalis has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, exhibiting activity against various microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and yeast, which can help prevent the growth of harmful pathogens and reduce the risk of infections.

The plant Salvia officinalis contains antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect cells from oxidative damage, neutralize free radicals, and reduce inflammation, potentially contributing to overall health and well-being.

Salvia officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
The plant Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used for its analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort, although the exact mechanisms of action are not fully understood.

Salvia officinalis has been found to exhibit pain-relieving properties, which may be attributed to its ability to modulate pain perception and reduce inflammation, providing relief from various types of pain, including headache and musculoskeletal pain.

The plant Salvia officinalis possesses antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and alleviate conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.

Salvia officinalis has been found to possess antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic responses.

The plant Salvia officinalis has been found to exhibit cardiovascular-protective properties, potentially contributing to the reduction of cardiovascular risk factors, such as high blood pressure, atherosclerosis, and cardiac arrhythmias, through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions.

Salvia officinalis has been found to possess neuroprotective properties, which may help protect against neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease, by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, and modulating neuroinflammatory responses.

constituents.description: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
A triterpenic acid that has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties
A flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.
Luteolin is a flavone with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
A flavonoid with antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties.
Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Salvia officinalis, characterized by their foaming properties, bitterness, and ability to emulsify oils.
Kaempferol is a flavonoid that exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Thymol is a phenolic monoterpene with antimicrobial, antiseptic, and antifungal properties.
Tannins in Salvia officinalis have astringent, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Salvia officinalis are a complex mixture of compounds, including rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, quercetin, luteolin, and apigenin. Rosmarinic acid is a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to have protective effects against oxidative stress and inflammation in various studies. Ursolic acid is a triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis in various research studies. Quercetin, luteolin, and apigenin are flavonoids with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, and have been found to have neuroprotective, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular-protective effects in multiple in vitro and in vivo studies. These compounds work synergistically to provide the medicinal properties of Salvia officinalis, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and cognitive impairment.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes are leaves. The leaves of Salvia officinalis contain primarily flavonoids such as apigenin and luteolin, and volatile oils including thujone and rosmarinic acid. The primary medicinal properties of the leaves include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids and volatile oils in the leaves have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-cancer properties, as well as to modulate the immune system. The rosmarinic acid present in the leaves has also been found to have anti-allergic and anti-histaminic effects.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in salvenic acid, carnosic acid, carnosol, and ursolic acid, which confer antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making them beneficial for reducing oxidative stress, alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression, and supporting the immune system, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, respiratory problems, and as a cognitive stimulant.
preparations.overview: Salvia officinalis tincture is a widely used herbal remedy prepared from the leaves and flowers of the Salvia officinalis plant, commonly known as sage. It is commonly used to treat a range of health issues including anxiety, inflammation, digestive issues such as indigestion and gastroenteritis, respiratory issues like cough and migraines. This preparation is also used to alleviate menstrual cramps and skin conditions. Due to its widespread use, Salvia officinalis tincture is considered a common and well-established herbal remedy. One of its most notable effects is its ability to reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom of many of the conditions it is used to treat. The preparation is made by soaking the leaves and flowers in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, and can be moderately difficult to produce, requiring a basic understanding of herbalism and some skill with the extraction process.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with 80-proof vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture and store it in a cool, dark place; take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat minor wounds, insect bites, and skin irritations, with applications in wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial therapy.
preparations.overview: Salvia officinalis salve is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat wounds, bruises, burns, skin irritation, insect bites, minor cuts, eczema, skin irritations and aid in wound healing and muscle strains. It is a widely used remedy, often found in homes and first aid kits due to its effectiveness. This salve typically has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The preparation is made by infusing leaves and flowers of the Salvia officinalis plant in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil. However, making this salve is not a simple process, as it requires precise measurement of the plant material and oil, as well as careful consideration of the infusion time to ensure the desired potency.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, 1/4 cup of dried Salvia officinalis flowers, and 1/2 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Combine the fresh leaves and dried flowers in a saucepan, and cover them with the carrier oil, then heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the cooled oil mixture, then stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the salve mixture into a clean glass jar, label it, and store it in a cool, dark place, using a small amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) as needed for topical application.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and as an antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.overview: Salvia officinalis tea is a common herbal remedy used to treat various health issues. It's often used to alleviate indigestion, inflammation, and digestive problems such as diarrhea and gastroenteritis. Additionally, it's commonly used to calm anxiety and stress, as well as to relieve respiratory issues like cough and insomnia. This preparation is extremely common, found in many households and used by people worldwide. A very common effect of Salvia officinalis tea is its ability to induce sleep due to its sedative properties. The tea is typically made by infusing the leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant in hot water, which is a simple and easy process.
recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 1/2 teaspoon of dried leaves for every 8 oz of water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-3 cups of the herbal tea per day, depending on individual needs and preferences.

preparations.description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
preparations.overview: Salvia officinalis essential oil is used to treat anxiety, stress, inflammation, digestive issues, insomnia, migraines, cough, wound healing, skin issues and respiratory issues. It's a widely used preparation, especially in aromatherapy, due to its popularity and availability. Its common effect is a sedative and calming action, which contributes to its use as a sleep aid. The oil is extracted from the leaves and flowers of Salvia officinalis, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The process of extraction is challenging, as it requires distillation or solvent extraction to obtain the essential oil, which is a concentrated and potent form of the plant's active compounds.
recipe: Gather 250g of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 50g of flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a cool, well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks, or until they reach a moisture content of 10-15%.
Steam distill the dried leaves and flowers using a ratio of 1:2, with the distillation time lasting around 2-3 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the distilled water, using a maximum of 1-2% of the total distilled volume.
Store the Salvia officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper cap, using a recommended dosage of 2-3 drops per application.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and indigestion, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function due to its antioxidant and neuroprotective properties.
preparations.overview: Salvia officinalis capsule is a widely used herbal preparation made from the leaves and flowers of the Salvia officinalis plant. It's commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, digestive issues, insomnia, stress, migraines, inflammation, sleep disorders, depression, and gastroesophageal reflux, as well as cough. This preparation is frequently used due to its effectiveness. One of its common effects is its ability to act as a sedative, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The leaves and flowers of the Salvia officinalis plant are used to make the capsule, which requires moderate difficulty to manufacture due to the need for precise processing and quality control to ensure the desired medicinal properties are retained.
recipe: Harvest fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers, then dry them thoroughly in a cool, dark place.
Grind the dried Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground Salvia officinalis powder with 1 part of a natural filler, such as rice flour, to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty herbal capsules with the Salvia officinalis blend, following the manufacturer's guidelines for filling and sealing.
Store the Salvia officinalis capsules in an airtight container and take 1-2 capsules, 2-3 times a day, as needed, with a glass of water.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion. Salves, made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil, are employed topically to soothe sore muscles and reduce inflammation. Salvia officinalis tea is commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and to aid in the management of menopausal hot flashes. Essential oil, extracted through steam distillation, is used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels. Salvia officinalis capsules, which contain dried and powdered leaves, are utilized to support the health of the immune system.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Salvia officinalis ingestion can cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
Contact dermatitis, urticaria, and anaphylaxis occur due to hypersensitivity to Salvia officinalis.
Salvia officinalis contains salvinorin A, which can cause gastrointestinal upset and diarrhea due to its irritant effect on the intestinal mucosa.
Salvia officinalis essential oil inhalation causes a sudden drop in blood pressure, leading to dizziness.
Ingestion of excessive amounts of Salvia officinalis causes stomach discomfort, nausea, and vomiting.
High doses of Salvia officinalis essential oil cause headaches in some individuals due to its high thujone content, which is toxic and can cause seizures and death if ingested in large amounts.
Salvia officinalis ingestion causes a significant decrease in systolic blood pressure
Consuming excessive amounts of Salvia officinalis can cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to nausea.
Salvia officinalis essential oil may contain thujone, a toxic compound that can cause stomach ulcers.
High doses of Salvia officinalis essential oil cause emetic effects in humans.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Salvia officinalis are abdominal pain, which can be caused by consuming large amounts of the plant's essential oils. Allergic reaction, such as hives and itching, may also occur in individuals who are sensitive to its compounds. Diarrhea is a common gastrointestinal issue resulting from the plant's high salvinorin A content. Dizziness and gastrointestinal upset can occur due to the plant's ability to interact with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions, leading to a range of symptoms including headache and hypotension. Improper use can also cause nausea, stomach ulcer, and vomiting, particularly when consumed in excessive amounts or combined with other substances.

precautions.precaution_description: Pregnant women should consult a doctor before using Salvia officinalis due to potential effects on the fetus.
Salvia officinalis dosages should be based on 0.6-4.5 grams of dried leaves per day for medicinal use.
Salvia officinalis interacts with antacids, blood thinners, diabetes medications, digoxin, and thyroid medications, affecting their efficacy or increasing the risk of side effects.
Salvia officinalis may exacerbate existing kidney problems due to its thymine and saponin content, which are nephrotoxic.
Salvia officinalis contains salvinorin A, a potent terpene that may induce uterine contractions and stimulate the uterus, which can cause preterm labor and abortion.
Salvia officinalis extracts can lower blood pressure due to their effect on calcium channels and sympathetic nervous system activity.
Salvia officinalis inhibits lactation due to its estrogenic compounds.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Salvia officinalis medicinally are to consult a doctor to rule out any underlying health conditions, determine dosages carefully to avoid toxicity, and avoid interactions with other medications such as blood thinners, as it may increase the risk of bleeding, and monitor kidney function closely in individuals with kidney disease, as high doses can exacerbate renal impairment. Avoid pregnancy and breastfeeding, as Salvia officinalis may stimulate uterine contractions and potentially harm the fetus or infant. Individuals with high blood pressure should also monitor their blood pressure closely, as the herb may exacerbate hypertension. This is crucial for pregnant women, as it may lead to premature labor or other complications.
identification: Salvia officinalis is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its upright growth habit, grayish-green leaves with crenate margins, and terminal spikes of blue to purple flowers. Native to the Mediterranean region, this species has been naturalized in many parts of the world, including Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, typically growing in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade. The plant can grow up to 75 cm in height, with a spread of around 45 cm, and its leaves are often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes. Salvia officinalis is found in a variety of habitats, including dry rocky areas, scrublands, and woodland edges, and is often associated with calcareous soils. The plant's growth is adapted to survive dry periods, with a deep taproot allowing it to access water deep in the soil.
cultivation: Salvia officinalis is a hardy perennial herb that exhibits a shrub-like growth habit, typically growing 1-2 feet tall and 1-3 feet wide. It prefers well-drained soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, full sun to partial shade, and moderate watering, requiring around 20-40 inches of annual rainfall. Growth tips include pruning back established plants in late spring to promote bushy growth, dividing and replanting every 3-4 years to maintain vigor, and providing protection from extreme cold and wet conditions. Salvia officinalis is also known to be drought-tolerant and can thrive in areas with low fertility, making it a suitable choice for gardeners with challenging soil conditions. Regular harvesting of the leaves in the summer months can encourage new growth and prevent the plant from flowering, allowing for continuous use throughout the growing season.
history: Salvia officinalis is an herb that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with the ancient Greeks and Romans utilizing it to treat a variety of ailments, including indigestion, melancholy, and sore throats. The herb contains compounds such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In the Middle Ages, Salvia officinalis was used to treat conditions such as epilepsy, gout, and ulcers, often in combination with other herbs. The plant's leaves and flowers were also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant properties. Historical records also suggest that Salvia officinalis was used to treat fever and even the plague.
